title: linum angustifolium var. ambiguum
intro: Linum angustifolium var. ambiguum, also known as narrow-leaved flax or blue flax, is a plant species within the flax family that is native to certain regions of North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Linum angustifolium var. ambiguum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to support skin health and reduce the risk of infection. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and leaves in a salve or ointment to aid in wound care and skin health.
